category_title: Struggling Chamorro readers and writers in collaborative settings
minimal_context: The students are in a small group practicing reading and retelling the tale 'The Old Lady in the Vinegar Bottle' to one another.
dialogue:
TUTOR: It is time to practice reading and retelling 'The Old Lady in the Vinegar Bottle' to one another in your small group.

STUDENT: It's not your turn, it's my turn, it's not your turn.

TUTOR: Please decide who will go first.

STUDENT: Okay whose turn? Your turn.

TUTOR: Listen quietly while he begins to retell the story.

STUDENT: Juan, you're supposed to start from the beginning.

options.A: Introduce the retelling strategy with a familiar text, selecting favorite island legends and other tales for the students to read and retell.
options.B: Allow the student to select a positive reinforcer before beginning the retelling activity, providing it when they finish the task or make an honest attempt.
options.C: Reduce the overall size of the reading and retelling task so the student can successfully complete it during the small group session.
options.D: Gradually fade and decrease the amount of scaffolding provided during the retelling activity as the student's competence with the story increases.
